content.zh:
Helicopter Cube Tutorial (2)

Yujian Song

In this tutorial, we are going to talk about the way to solve jumble Helicopter Cube.

Rotating Regulation

In the Helicopter, each edge can do jumble rotations after rotating the angle of arccos1/3 (about 70.5 degrees) clockwise or counterclockwise. In this tutorial, I will add the sign “+” (or “-”) after each edge to present this edge rotating arccos1/3 clockwise or counterclockwise, and rotating 180° if without any sign. For example:

UF

UF+

UF-

UF UF+

UF UF-

Step 1: Cubeshape

When solving many jumble-able puzzles, the first step is always cubeshape (may not necessarily be “cube shape”, depending on the shape of its solved state), the same to the Helicopter. “Cubeshape” here means letting the Helicopter become the non-jumble state, also means making the shape of Helicopter a cube.

Here is the simplest jumble operate below: UF+ FR UF-

We can see that this operate let the two groups of center and corner blocks reverse, so when meeting two reversed block groups like the example above, we can rotate them to the position above, then do UF+ FR UF- to solve them.

For the example below, there are two reversed center blocks. We can do UR+ FL+ UF+ UR- FL- first to make it the state with two reversed block groups, then do UR UB to make it the standard state above, and finally do UB- UL UB+ to solve it.

So we get the formula below:

UR+ FL+ UF+ UR- FL- UR UB UB- UL UB+

The state of two reversed center blocks is very common when solving the Helicopter. Here are some setup formulas of them. (All formulas below are easy to understand, so the following formulas are only for simple listing.)

Setup on the same edge:

UR+ FL+ UF+ UR- FL- UL- FR- UF+ UL+ FR+

Setup to the free neighboring edge:

UR+ UF UR UF+

Setup to the locked neighboring edge:

UF+ FR UF FR+

In actual operation, we may meet many pairs of reversed center blocks. Two pairs of reversed center blocks can be solved separately by the formulas above, and here is a shortcut formula for this state:

FL+ UF UR FL UF UR-

For the states of three pairs of reversed center blocks, here is a representative state below:

Setup the reversed blocks firstly:

Do x2 y', then setup again:

Do y x2 to let uf-dl pairs cancel each other out, then solve the last pair:

This state of three reversed block pairs can lead to a very classic pattern: Mezon Pattern.

Doing UF- BR- DL- from the state below, we can get the shape of the Mezon Pattern, just like reversing two corner blocks:

Further, solving other blocks, this will make people mistake that there are only two corner blocks reversed for 180°. This is exactly the Mezon Pattern.

Mezon Pattern. image from TwistyPuzzles

It is a challenging process to solve the Helicopter to this pattern. Because of this, some one created the Mezon Helicopter, which makes the Mezon Pattern as its solved state. Doing a Mezon Pattern again can let it be back to the simple Helicopter puzzle.

Mezon Helicopter, image from TwistyPuzzles

Let’s back to the simple Helicopter. Using the methods and examples above, we can finish the cubeshape process of the Helicopter.

Step 2: Solving it as non-jumble Helicopter (HeliDoctre)

In this step, though I call it “as non-jumble Helicopter”, there still some differences in fact. The cause of differences is that jumble rotating breaks the four clusters of center blocks, allowing a block moving from a cluster to another cluster, and this effect cannot be corrected by simple cubeshape process.

For example, a simplest way to break clusters is:

UR+ FL+ UF UR- FL-

It is easy to see that this formula exchange the red center block and the orange one, but this two blocks do not belong to the same cluster, just like the image below:

The blocks circled, which do not belong to the same cluster, are exchanged by the formula above.

So when solving the Helicopter, we should use this formula flexibly to move center blocks to their own clusters. Here is a solving example below: (Cubeshape process is omitted.)

We still solve the four center blocks of one face first. In this step, we may need to exchange blocks in different clusters. For the example below, the white block is not in the correct cluster, so we should do UF- BR- UR UF+ BR+ to move it in the right cluster, then do UF UB UR UF to solve it.

Solving the four corner blocks is same as the HeliDoctre, omitted here.

Then solve the center blocks on the side faces of the bottom layer. As the image below, if need to solve the orange center block, we can do FL- UB- UL FL+ UB+ first to correct its cluster, then do UB FR UF UR UF FR to solve it.

The next step is solving the rest center blocks. It can be done easily by using the commutator formulas of the HeliDoctre and cluster-exchanging formula above.

For example, in the image below, the orange center block can be moved to its correct cluster by doing UF UL UL+ BR+ UB UL- BR-.

Solve the blue center block: UB UB+ FR+ UR UB- FR-.

Do UR FR:

Do UB UB+ FR+ UR UB- FR-:

Then we need to exchange the yellow block and the green block in the image below. We can do UR UF UL to setup them to the ready position of the cluster-exchanging formula first, then do the cluster-exchanging formula that UL+ BR+ UB UL- BR-, and finally solve the scrambled blocks using UL UF UB UR.

Then we find that there are two center blocks (blue & yellow) should be exchanged in the same cluster, which cannot happen in the HeliDoctre. The reason is that there are one pair (or more) center blocks with the same color being in wrong clusters. For example, we can setup the two red blocks by doing BR UB UL, then do cluster-exchanging formula that UL- FR- UF UL+ FR+, and finally do reversing setup that UL UB BR to solve it.

With further thinking of the cluster-exchanging formula, if we change the formula above to a new one that FL+ UR+ UF FL- UR- UF, the corner blocks on the bottom layer will not be scrambled. So we get the new formula below:

BR UB UL FL+ UR+ UF FL- UR- UF UL UB BR

Using the steps above (1.letting all blocks to correct clusters; 2.solving all blocks in their own cluster), we can solve all center blocks of the Helicopter. This solving method is very useful when solving jumble-able puzzles.

Finally, solve corner blocks in the top layer. This step can totally use the method of HeliDoctre. If using jumble rotation is allowed, the formula of double-corner switching can be more easier:

UL- FR- UF UL+ FR+ FL+ UR+ UF FL- UR- UF

This formula is easier to remember than the old one: UF FR UR UF FR UF UR FR UR UF UR UF UR.

And now, the Helicopter is solved.
